title: Oreostemma peirsonii
intro: Oreostemma peirsonii, also known as Peirson's marigold or desert marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreostemma peirsonii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support the immune system.
taxonomy: Oreostemma peirsonii is classified as a species within the family Compositae. This flowering plant is a member of the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The Compositae family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants in the world. Oreostemma peirsonii is often found growing in the mountainous regions of California, where it can thrive in areas with well-drained soil and full sun to partial shade. Its classification also places it within the tribe Madinae, a group of plants known for their distinctive flower structures and growth habits.
uses: Oreostemma peirsonii, also known as Peirson's sunflower, is a plant native to the western United States.
